title: Newborn Skin Development Structure and Function

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Newborn Skin Development?
ผิวหนัง = organ ที่ใหญ่ที่สุดของร่างกาย ทำหน้าที่เป็น primary barrier ต่อ infection, thermoregulation, และ electrolyte homeostasis. ใน ELBW infant ผิวหนังเป็น organ ที่ fragile และ at-risk ที่สุด — stratum corneum ยังไม่ mature จนถึง 33 weeks. สิ่งสำคัญ 5 ข้อ: (1) premature skin: TEWL สูง, percutaneous absorption สูง → ยา/สารพิษ topical เป็นอันตรายจริง; (2) birthmarks + neurologic implications — large melanocytic nevus → NCM (MRI ต้องทำ), capillary malformation → Sturge-Weber, segmental hemangioma → PHACE; (3) Blaschko lines ≠ dermatomes — ต้องแยกให้ออก; (4) early emollients: benefit ใน barrier function แต่ prophylactic emollient ใน VLBW อาจ ↑ nosocomial infection; (5) filaggrin mutations → atopy triad (eczema + asthma + allergies) — epicutaneous sensitization pathway.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
รู้ stages ของ fetal skin development ตั้งแต่ ectoderm → epidermis: embryonic (5–8 wk) → fetal (9 wk – birth), ระบุ milestones สำคัญ
อธิบาย dermoepidermal junction (DEJ) — components (hemidesmosomes, anchoring filaments, type VII collagen) และ epidermolysis bullosa
รู้ dermis development: collagen (15 wk), elastin (22–24 wk), vasculogenesis (complete 20 wk), adipose (2nd trimester); Goltz syndrome
อธิบาย appendage development: hair (12 wk), nails (8 wk), sebaceous glands (13–16 wk), vernix formation, eccrine sweat glands; ectodermal dysplasias
แยก Blaschko lines จาก dermatomes — clinical significance ใน neonatal skin diagnosis
ระบุ impact ของ prematurity ต่อ skin: TEWL, acid mantle, percutaneous absorption; เปรียบ premature vs term skin
รู้ reported hazards ของ percutaneous absorption ใน newborn: compound, product, และ toxicity
วิเคราะห์ evidence เรื่อง emollients ใน VLBW (prophylactic) vs older preterm vs atopy prevention — ผลต่างกัน
ใช้ morphologic approach ในการ classify neonatal skin lesions: macule, papule, vesicle, pustule, wheal
สร้าง differential diagnosis สำหรับ vesicopustular eruptions, blueberry muffin baby, erythroderma, collodion membrane
รู้ birthmarks ที่มี neurologic implications: large melanocytic nevi → NCM, OSD stigmata, Sturge-Weber, PHACE — แต่ละอัน imaging ที่ใช้
1. Fetal Skin Development — จาก Ectoderm สู่ Barrier
1.1 Epidermis
Epidermis derives entirely from ectoderm. Single-layered epithelium spans the embryo from gastrulation. Development proceeds in discrete stages.

rows: Single-layered ectoderm; expression of epidermal keratins at 6 wk
DEJ begins forming; specialized cells (melanocytes, Langerhans) appear
Differentiation; collagen fibers accumulate in dermis; hair canals form
Skin first becomes functional; stratum corneum matures; vernix forms

blocks.text: Stratum corneum = 'bricks and mortar': anucleate corneocytes (bricks) + cells, adhesion proteins, lipids (mortar). Keratinization เริ่มที่ head, face, palms ก่อน

Filaggrin (filament aggregating protein): mutations → ↓ moisture retention → fissures → epicutaneous allergen exposure + sensitization → atopy triad. Palmer Nat Genet 2006: major predisposing factor for atopic dermatitis
1.2 Dermoepidermal Junction (DEJ)
DEJ ทำหน้าที่ยึด epidermis กับ dermis + ต้านทาน shearing forces. Critical elements form by 8–10 weeks' gestation
Components: hemidesmosomes → anchoring filaments → type IV collagen (lamina densa) → anchoring fibrils (type VII collagen) — tethers dermis to epidermis

Epidermolysis Bullosa (EB) — DEJ Disorder
EB = inherited cutaneous fragility จาก defective DEJ proteins. Subtype ขึ้นกับ protein ที่ defective: (1) EB Simplex — keratin 5/14 (suprabasal); (2) Junctional EB — laminin-332, collagen XVII, integrins (within DEJ); (3) Dystrophic EB — type VII collagen (anchoring fibrils, below DEJ). Blistering at friction-prone sites (hands, feet): handle gently — avoid adhesives, leads, monitors ที่ trigger blistering. Cannot determine subtype from clinical exam alone — genetic testing needed.
1.3 Dermis and Subcutis
Dermis derives from mesoderm; embryonic mesenchymal cells → fibroblasts, vascular cells, adipocytes, Langerhans cells

Timeline: collagen fibers accumulate by 15 weeks; elastin fibers detectable by EM 22–24 weeks; immature dermis will NOT scar when traumatized (e.g., fetal skin biopsy) — scar potential develops ~end of 2nd trimester

Vasculogenesis (endothelial cell formation from angioblasts) = complete by 20 weeks; angiogenesis follows. Two types: sprouting (grows toward VEGF/bFGF) and intussusceptive (vessel splits in two — only in pre-existing vascularity)
Neural networks: develop end of 1st trimester, follow vascular pattern. Adipose: beneath dermis, from 2nd trimester
Goltz syndrome (focal dermal hypoplasia): X-linked dominant; PORCN mutations; 90% female; linear atrophic plaques + fat herniation from dermal atrophy
1.4 Appendages
Dermal–epidermal interaction commences ~10 weeks → hair follicle, nail, sweat gland, apocrine gland formation

Hair: follicle formation initiates in dermis → directs overlying basal cells → placode; differentiation 2nd trimester; hair canals form 20 weeks; hair cycles: anagen (growth) → catagen (resting) → telogen (shedding); 1st shedding cycle at 28 weeks; final shedding cycle: postnatal at occiput → occipital alopecia (often misattributed to pressure)
Nails: begin at 8 weeks; fully keratinized nail plate by 5 months' EGA; nail keratins have greater structural integrity than epidermal keratins

Sebaceous glands: develop contemporaneously with hair follicles; bulges seen 13–16 weeks above arrector pili muscle insertion; maternal hormones induce sebum production (late 2nd + 3rd trimesters); sebum + shed corneocytes form vernix caseosa — hydrophobic, hydrating, antimicrobial (lysozyme + lactoferrin); cephalocaudal orientation; fetal lung maturity parallels sebaceous gland activity (surfactant emulsifies vernix)
Eccrine sweat glands: palmoplantar at 55–65 days, fully developed by 2nd trimester; interfollicular + apocrine glands: mid-2nd trimester
Hydrotic ectodermal dysplasia (Clouston syndrome): GJB6 mutations (connexin 30) → gap junction defects in adnexal structures; nail dystrophy + alopecia + palmoplantar hyperkeratosis
1.5 Specialized Epidermal Cells

rows: Melanin synthesis; migrate along Blaschko lines; skin not fully pigmented at birth → darkens in first months

blocks.text: 2. Blaschko Lines — แยกจาก Dermatomes
Blaschko lines = patterns of epidermal cell migration during embryogenesis; reflect clonal mosaicism along the path of migration of skin cells from neural crest to epidermis
Dermatomes = follow peripheral sensory nerve distribution; determined by spinal cord segment

Blaschko Lines vs Dermatomes — Clinical Relevance
Cutaneous findings along Blaschko lines = clue to underlying mutational event (somatic mosaicism), NOT a nerve-based process. Classic example: Incontinentia pigmenti (NEMO mutations, X-linked dominant): vesicles → warty hyperkeratosis → dyspigmentation — all along Blaschko lines. Herpes zoster = vesicles in a dermatomal distribution. ความแตกต่าง: Blaschko = S-shaped + streaks along trunk; Dermatomes = band-like around trunk following spinal level.
3. Impact of Prematurity ต่อผิวหนัง
3.1 Stratum Corneum Maturity
Stratum corneum reaches functional maturity ~33 weeks' EGA. Preterm infant < 33 wk = developmentally + functionally incompetent barrier
Risk combination in ELBW: (1) ineffective barrier + (2) absent/diminished vernix + (3) large BSA:mass ratio + (4) immature immune system → ↑↑ risk invasive infection + skin injury
Unique risk: percutaneous toxicity — topical medications, cleansers, even emollients (e.g., lactic acid) → dangerous systemic levels in premature infants
3.2 Premature vs Term Skin — เปรียบเทียบ
3.3 Reported Hazards of Percutaneous Absorption
ตาราง 91.3 จาก Avery รวม compounds ที่มีรายงาน toxicity จาก percutaneous absorption ใน newborn:

⚠️ EMLA ใน Preterm — Methemoglobinemia Risk
Prilocaine (component ของ EMLA) → methemoglobinemia ใน premature infant ผ่าน percutaneous absorption. Minimizing pain is important แต่ต้องระวัง safe limits. G6PD deficiency ↑ susceptibility. ยาร่วมที่ ↑ risk: sulfonamides, acetaminophen, phenobarbital, phenytoin. Use safe topical anesthetic: limit application area + time, avoid in ELBW < 26 weeks.
4. Care of Newborn Skin — Evidence-Based
4.1 Care of Preterm Skin
Thermoregulation: humidified incubator จำกัด evaporative water loss; gentle drying at delivery; skin barrier immaturity → adhesives/alcohol/iodine cause injury
Specialized dressings: DuoDerm, Mepilex, Vigilon — ปกป้อง vulnerable sites (ET tube, umbilical catheter); useful ใน mechanobullous diseases (EB)
Frequent repositioning + appropriate bedding → prevent pressure necrosis

Prophylactic Emollients ใน VLBW — Evidence Inconclusive
Edwards WH et al. Pediatrics 2004;113:1195 (Vermont Oxford Network): prophylactic ointment therapy ใน BW 501–1000 g → ↑ nosocomial sepsis rate compared to control. ดังนั้น prophylactic emollients ไม่ recommended broadly ใน VLBW — อาจ benefit เฉพาะบาง infant ที่ high-risk skin breakdown. Contrast กับ older preterm (>29 wk) ที่ emollients enhance barrier development (Kiechl-Kohlendorfer Pediatr Dermatol 2008).
4.2 General Care of Newborn Skin

Emollients in atopy prevention: Simpson EL et al. J Allergy Clin Immunol 2014;134:818: ↓ incidence of atopic dermatitis by 50% at 6 months; แต่ Chalmers JR et al. (BEEP trial) Lancet 2020;395:962: daily emollient did NOT validate atopy prevention — evidence remains uncertain

Sunflower seed oil: Darmstadt GL et al. Lancet 2005;365:1039: ↓ nosocomial infections by 41% in preterm infants < 33 wk (Bangladesh RCT). Safflower, sesame, coconut, apricot oil = fatty acids with anti-inflammatory + antibacterial properties
Olive oil: avoid — can promote Pityrosporum growth → suboptimal choice for skin care
Bathing: WHO recommends delay beyond 6 hours after birth in high-risk settings (preserves vernix, thermoregulation)
Skin pH at birth: alkaline (6.5–7.5) → declines to 4.0–5.5 in first week (establishes 'acid mantle'); alkaline soaps disrupt and delay acid mantle → use gentle, pH-neutral, fragrance-free cleansers

Hand hygiene: chlorhexidine preferred (bacterial decontamination); 10% povidone–iodine advantageous for Candida; chlorhexidine 0.5% superior to povidone–iodine for peripheral catheter colonization reduction in neonates
Chlorhexidine for umbilical stump care: most effective agent to prevent omphalitis and sepsis
5. Morphologic Approach to Neonatal Skin Lesions
เริ่มต้น skin examination ด้วยการ identify primary morphology — ลด differential diagnosis + หลีกเลี่ยง confusion จาก secondary findings (erosions, ulcerations)

rows: Raised, sloping border, no epidermal change

blocks.text: 6. Important Clinical Presentations ที่ Neonatologist ต้องรู้
6.1 Collodion Membrane

'Collodion baby': tight plastic wrap–like encasement on skin at birth; taut skin → eclabium (lip eversion) + ectropion (eyelid eversion); dysregulation of fluids, electrolytes, thermoregulation → NICU care ใน controlled environment

Membrane sheds spontaneously over first weeks; majority → chronic skin disorder — most commonly autosomal recessive congenital ichthyosis; others: Gaucher disease, ectodermal dysplasia; rarely lamellar exfoliation of the newborn (self-resolving, normal skin)
Diagnosis: skin biopsy and/or genetic testing; Prado R J Am Acad Dermatol 2012;67:1362
6.2 Vesicopustular and Bullous Eruptions
Widespread vesicles/pustules/bullae/erosions in newborn = daunting differential. Treatable infectious causes first.
Rule out infection first: HSV (cultures + DFA), varicella, Staphylococcus aureus, Candida; Group B Strep, Listeria, Pseudomonas, Treponema pallidum
Erythema toxicum neonatorum: smear → eosinophils; very common, benign, transient
Transient neonatal pustular melanosis (TNPM): smear → neutrophils; common in pigmented skin; leaves hyperpigmented macules

Epidermolysis bullosa: blistering at friction-prone areas (hands, feet); early awareness → avoid iatrogenic exacerbation (adhesives, leads). Cannot determine EB subtype clinically; relatively mild blistering at birth ≠ rule out severe subtype
6.3 Blueberry Muffin Baby
Red–purple macules/papules/plaques; historically = TORCH workup: Toxoplasmosis, Other, Rubella, CMV, HSV
Add noninfectious differential: Langerhans cell histiocytosis, neuroblastoma, leukemia cutis, transient myeloproliferative disorder, mastocytosis, multifocal vascular lesions
Skin biopsy may be necessary to discriminate; Avram MM et al. N Engl J Med 2007;357:1327
6.4 Erythroderma
Diffuse erythema ± scaling; newborn skin extremely reactive — wait to distinguish physiologic (cutis marmorata) from pathology
DDx: infectious (rule out first) → genetic/inflammatory (ichthyosis, psoriasis, seborrheic dermatitis); metabolic disorders, primary immunodeficiency, autoinflammatory syndromes
Menkes disease: copper metabolism disorder; neonatal erythroderma as first manifestation; timely intervention (copper histidinate) can mitigate morbidity; Galve J et al. Pediatrics 2012;130:e239
7. Birthmarks With Neurologic Implications
7.1 Large Congenital Melanocytic Nevi → NCM
Large congenital melanocytic nevus (LCMN) → risk of neurocutaneous melanosis (NCM) — melanocytic proliferation in leptomeninges
NCM risk ↑ if: nevus on scalp or midline, or multiple (>20) satellite nevi
Workup: MRI brain + spine with and without contrast necessary to rule out NCM

Feed-and-Wrap MRI — Window ของ Early Infancy
Early infancy offers a unique window for unsedated MRI using 'feed and wrap' technique: feed infant → swaddle → image during sleep. Antonov NK et al. Clin Pediatr 2017;56:1095: feasible in infants, avoids unnecessary general anesthesia. Awareness ของ window นี้สำคัญมาก — ถ้าพลาดช่วงนี้ infant จะต้องได้รับ general anesthesia สำหรับ MRI ในภายหลัง.
7.2 Occult Spinal Dysraphism (OSD)
Cutaneous stigmata ที่บ่งชี้ OSD: pigmented lesions, vascular lesions, excessive hair (hairy patch), abnormal dimpling/clefting in midline sacrum
↑ Risk ถ้า: 2+ such lesions, dimples > 5 mm at base, หรือ farther than 2.5 cm from anus
Imaging: spinal ultrasound before complete vertebral ossification (false-negative rate high); Kriss VM, Desai NS. AJR 1998;171:1687
7.3 Sturge-Weber Syndrome
Facial capillary malformation (port wine stain) → ipsilateral angiomatosis + glaucoma; MRI with contrast: leptomeningeal malformation → ↑ seizure risk + developmental delay
CT: 'tram-track calcifications' in temporal + occipital cortex (may not be present initially)
7.4 PHACE Syndrome
Segmental infantile hemangioma (typically face) → associated with:
P — Posterior fossa brain malformations (Dandy-Walker, cerebellar dysgenesis)
H — Hemangioma (large segmental, face)
A — Arterial anomalies (tortuous, dysmorphic cerebral vessels → stroke risk)
C — Cardiovascular anomalies (aortic coarctation)
E — Eye abnormalities
Imaging: MRI with angiography — tortuous/dysmorphic cerebral vessels diagnostic; Garzon MC et al. J Pediatr 2016;178:24 (consensus guidelines)
8. NICU Practical — Skin Care ในทารก NICU
ELBW < 26 weeks: humidified incubator ทันที; gentle drying; หลีกเลี่ยง alcohol + iodine บน skin โดยตรง; ใช้ specialized dressings ที่ adhesive sites (DuoDerm/Tegaderm)
Topical agents — principle: ทุก topical agent ที่ใช้ใน preterm ต้องประเมิน percutaneous absorption risk; avoid hexachlorophene; ระวัง EMLA ใน < 30 wk + G6PD deficiency + co-medications
Emollient protocol: ไม่ routine prophylactic ใน VLBW < 29 wk (↑ sepsis risk); ใช้ได้ใน > 29 wk สำหรับ barrier enhancement; sunflower seed oil = preferred option ถ้า available
Umbilical cord care: chlorhexidine solution — proven ↓ omphalitis + sepsis (WHO high-risk settings)
Bathing: defer initial bath จนกว่า temperature stable; ไม่ใช้ cleansers ใน 2 สัปดาห์แรก; warm water + cotton pledgets; surface cleanse ≤ 2×/week

Collodion baby at birth: NICU controlled environment; monitor fluids + electrolytes + thermoregulation; membrane sheds spontaneously; genetic workup (skin biopsy + genetic testing) สำหรับ underlying diagnosis

Birthmarks: large melanocytic nevus scalp/midline หรือ >20 satellite → feed-and-wrap MRI ก่อน 3 เดือน; facial capillary malformation → ophthalmology (glaucoma) + neurology; segmental hemangioma face → MRI with angiography (PHACE)
Vesicular/bullous eruptions: rule out HSV/infection before attributing to benign cause; erythema toxicum (eosinophils) vs TNPM (neutrophils) — smear ช่วย; EB = handle gently, avoid adhesives, leads
9. 5 Bedside Pearls — ผิวหนัง NICU

PEARL 1 — Stratum Corneum Mature ที่ 33 Weeks — ไม่ใช่ Term
Stratum corneum functional maturity = ~33 weeks, ไม่ใช่ term. ดังนั้น infant 33–36 wk ก็ยังมี partially immature barrier. TEWL ที่ 26 wk = 45 g/m²/hr (term = 5 g/m²/hr) → evaporative water loss ทำให้เกิด dehydration + electrolyte imbalance ได้เร็วมาก. Use humidified incubator + monitor electrolytes closely in all < 33 wk.

PEARL 2 — Olive Oil ≠ Good Skin Oil
ผู้ปกครองมักใช้ olive oil กับลูก — แต่ olive oil ส่งเสริมการเจริญเติบโตของ Pityrosporum → ไม่ใช่ optimal choice สำหรับ neonatal skin care. Preferred: sunflower seed oil, safflower, coconut, apricot — contain fatty acids with anti-inflammatory + antibacterial properties. แนะนำ parent ทุกรายที่ถามเรื่อง baby massage oil.

PEARL 3 — Blaschko Lines ≠ Dermatomes: อย่าสับสน
Blaschko lines = embryonic migration path ของ melanocytes; ไม่มีความสัมพันธ์กับ nerve segments. ดังนั้น ถ้าผื่น vesicular along Blaschko lines → incontinentia pigmenti (genetic). ถ้าผื่น vesicular along dermatome → herpes zoster (infection). แยกให้ออก: Blaschko = S-shaped streaks ตามลำตัว; Dermatomes = band horizontal ตาม spinal level.

PEARL 4 — Occipital Alopecia ≠ Pressure; Feed-and-Wrap = Window
Occipital alopecia ใน newborn เกิดจาก final hair telogen shedding cycle — postnatal physiologic; ไม่ใช่ pressure/friction (บอก parent อย่าเปลี่ยนท่านอน). คนละเรื่องกับ alopecia areata. เรื่อง neuroimaging: feed-and-wrap technique ใน early infancy = opportunity ทอง สำหรับ unsedated MRI — ถ้า LCMN ต้องทำ NCM workup, ทำใน 2–3 เดือนแรก.

PEARL 5 — Blueberry Muffin Baby: TORCH ไม่พอ — Add Malignant Differential
Classic 'blueberry muffin baby' = red-purple macules/papules/plaques at birth. TORCH workup สำคัญแต่ไม่พอ — ต้อง add noninfectious differential: Langerhans cell histiocytosis, leukemia cutis, neuroblastoma, transient myeloproliferative disorder, mastocytosis. Skin biopsy อาจจำเป็น เพื่อ discriminate. อย่าให้ TORCH negative ทำให้หยุด workup — malignant causes ต้องการ early diagnosis.
10. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Palmer CNA et al. Nat Genet 2006;38:441: Common loss-of-function variants of filaggrin (FLG) are major predisposing factor for atopic dermatitis. Establishes filaggrin mutations as atopy triad gateway via epicutaneous sensitization.

Simpson EL et al. J Allergy Clin Immunol 2014;134:818: Early emollient application from birth (SCORAD 2-year follow-up) → ↓ atopic dermatitis incidence by 50% at 6 months in high-risk infants. Proposed epicutaneous barrier hypothesis.

Chalmers JR et al. (BEEP Trial) Lancet 2020;395:962: RCT, daily emollient during infancy does NOT prevent eczema at 2 years. Conflicts with Simpson 2014 — current evidence uncertain; do not broadly recommend for atopy prevention.

Edwards WH et al. (Vermont Oxford Network) Pediatrics 2004;113:1195: Prophylactic ointment therapy in BW 501–1000 g → higher nosocomial sepsis rate vs control. Prophylactic emollients not broadly recommended in VLBW.

Darmstadt GL et al. Lancet 2005;365:1039: Topical sunflower seed oil in preterm infants < 33 wk in Bangladesh RCT → ↓ nosocomial infection by 41%. Sunflower oil preferred over olive oil for neonatal skin care.

Kiechl-Kohlendorfer U et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2008;25:174: Daily olive oil/lanolin emollient in preterm > 29 weeks → ↑ skin barrier development (enhances acid mantle formation). Benefit in older preterm, not VLBW.

Prado R et al. J Am Acad Dermatol 2012;67:1362: Collodion baby updated management review — NICU care in controlled environment, fluid/electrolyte monitoring, genetic workup, outcomes of underlying diagnoses.
Antonov NK et al. Clin Pediatr (Phila) 2017;56:1095: Feed-and-wrap MRI technique in infants — feasible, avoids GA, optimal window in early infancy for neuroimaging in birthmarks with neurologic risk.
Garzon MC et al. J Pediatr 2016;178:24: PHACE syndrome consensus-derived diagnosis and care recommendations — MRI with angiography criteria, ophthalmology, neurology, cardiology referrals.

Holbrook KA, Odland GF. J Invest Dermatol 1975;65:16: Classic EM characterization of fetal skin embryogenesis stages — still foundational reference for developmental milestones table (Table 91.1 Avery).
Avram MM et al. N Engl J Med 2007;357:1327: MGH case record — newborn with vesiculopustular skin lesions; comprehensive differential diagnosis of neonatal blistering disorders (Box 91.2 Avery).
Kriss VM, Desai NS. AJR Am J Roentgenol 1998;171:1687: Occult spinal dysraphism in neonates: high-risk cutaneous stigmata on sonography — dimple criteria, indications for imaging.

Visscher MO et al. Clin Dermatol 2015;33:271: Newborn infant skin physiology, development, and care — comprehensive review covering acid mantle pH changes, chlorhexidine vs povidone-iodine for umbilical care.
11. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Stratum corneum mature เมื่ออายุ EGA เท่าไหร่? ELBW < 26 wk มี TEWL เท่าไหร่ vs term?

ตอบ: Stratum corneum functional maturity = ~33 weeks' EGA. ELBW ที่ 26 wk: TEWL = 45 g/m²/hr; term = 5 g/m²/hr (9× ต่างกัน). ผลคือ dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, ↑ percutaneous absorption ทุก topical agent. ดังนั้น humidified incubator + จำกัด topical exposure ใน < 33 wk.
Q: Blaschko lines คืออะไร? แตกต่างจาก dermatomes อย่างไร? ยกตัวอย่าง disease 1 ข้อ

ตอบ: Blaschko lines = patterns of epidermal cell migration during embryogenesis — reflect clonal mosaicism (somatic mutation). ไม่เกี่ยวกับ nerve. Dermatomes = spinal nerve territory. Example: Incontinentia pigmenti (NEMO mutations): vesicles → warty lesions → dyspigmentation ล้วน along Blaschko lines; X-linked dominant; multi-system (eyes, CNS, teeth). Contrast: herpes zoster = vesicles along dermatome (horizontal band).
Q: ผู้ปกครองถาม: ควรใช้ olive oil กับ newborn skin ไหม? ตอบอย่างไร?

ตอบ: แนะนำ ไม่ใช้ olive oil — olive oil สามารถ promote Pityrosporum (Malassezia) growth → suboptimal for neonatal skin. Siegfried & Glenn, Arch Pediatr Adolesc Med 2012. แนะนำ sunflower seed oil, safflower, coconut, หรือ apricot oil แทน — contain beneficial fatty acids (anti-inflammatory + antibacterial). Darmstadt 2005: sunflower seed oil ↓ nosocomial infection 41% in preterm infants.
Q: Collodion baby — immediate management คืออะไร? ส่วนใหญ่ underlying diagnosis คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Immediate management: NICU controlled environment (humidified isolette); monitor fluids, electrolytes, thermoregulation closely; membrane sheds spontaneously over first weeks. Underlying diagnoses: majority → autosomal recessive congenital ichthyosis; others: Gaucher disease, ectodermal dysplasia; rarely → lamellar exfoliation of newborn (self-resolving, normal skin outcome). Workup: skin biopsy + genetic testing (Prado J Am Acad Dermatol 2012).
Q: PHACE syndrome คือ acronym ของอะไร? Investigation ที่ต้องทำ?

ตอบ: P=Posterior fossa anomalies (Dandy-Walker); H=Hemangioma (large segmental face); A=Arterial anomalies (tortuous/dysmorphic cerebral vessels); C=Cardiovascular (aortic coarctation); E=Eye abnormalities. Investigation: MRI with MR angiography — looking for tortuous, dysmorphic cerebral vessels (can predispose to stroke); ophthalmology exam; cardiac evaluation for coarctation. Feed-and-wrap unsedated MRI ใน early infancy = window ทอง. Garzon MC et al. J Pediatr 2016;178:24 = consensus guidelines.
Q: Why is prophylactic emollient NOT broadly recommended in VLBW infants?

ตอบ: Edwards WH et al. Pediatrics 2004;113:1195 (Vermont Oxford Network RCT): prophylactic ointment in BW 501–1000 g → higher nosocomial sepsis rate vs control. กลไก: emollient occlusion + warm moist environment → bacterial overgrowth. Contrast กับ older preterm (>29 wk): Kiechl-Kohlendorfer 2008 showed ↑ barrier development. Takeaway: prophylactic emollients = individualised decision ใน VLBW, ไม่ใช่ routine.
Q: DEJ components คืออะไร? EB แบ่งตาม affected protein อย่างไร?

ตอบ: DEJ: hemidesmosomes (basal keratinocyte) → anchoring filaments (laminin-332, collagen XVII) → lamina lucida → lamina densa (type IV collagen) → anchoring fibrils (type VII collagen). EB classification by level of split: EB Simplex = keratin 5/14 (suprabasal split); Junctional EB = laminin-332, collagen XVII, integrins (DEJ split); Dystrophic EB = type VII collagen (sub-DEJ split). Outcome varies widely by subtype. Cannot determine subtype clinically — genetic testing required.
Q: Cutaneous stigmata อะไรบ้างที่ indicate ↑ risk ของ occult spinal dysraphism (OSD)?

ตอบ: OSD stigmata: pigmented lesion, vascular lesion, hairy patch, abnormal dimpling/clefting in midline sacrum. ↑ Risk ถ้า: 2+ stigmata, dimple > 5 mm at base, หรือ farther than 2.5 cm from anus. Imaging: spinal ultrasound ก่อน vertebral ossification สมบูรณ์ (false-negative rate high — MRI ถ้า abnormal/high suspicion). Kriss & Desai AJR 1998.
Q: Vernix caseosa ประกอบด้วยอะไร? ทำไม WHO แนะนำ delayed bathing?

ตอบ: Vernix = water + protein + lipids; hydrophobic + hydrating + antimicrobial (lysozyme + lactoferrin). Formed by sebum + shed corneocytes from 3rd trimester. WHO recommends delay bathing beyond 6 hours in high-risk settings: (1) preserve vernix antimicrobial protection; (2) prevent heat loss/hypothermia; (3) allow acid mantle establishment — newborn pH 6.5–7.5 needs time to fall to 4.0–5.5.
Day 92 Preview

Avery Chapter 92 — Congenital and Hereditary Disorders of the Skin
พรุ่งนี้: Congenital and Hereditary Disorders of the Skin — ต่อเนื่องจาก skin development (Ch91) สู่ genetic/structural skin disease: ichthyoses, epidermolysis bullosa (in depth), genodermatoses, incontinentia pigmenti, และ inherited disorders affecting skin appendages. เนื้อหาจะ connect กับ DEJ biology, Blaschko lines, และ filaggrin pathway ที่เรียนวันนี้ — การ understand Ch91 จะทำให้ Ch92 clear ขึ้นมาก.
